title: anthemis palaestina subsp. amblyolepis
intro: Anthemis palaestina subsp. amblyolepis, also known as Palestinian chamomile, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is used in herbal medicine for its calming and soothing properties, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and promoting better sleep, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Anthemis palaestina subsp. amblyolepis can be prepared for use in herbal remedies through infusions, tinctures, and topical applications.
